reasoning: The question requires predicting if the GISTEMP global temperature anomaly for October 2025 will fall within a specific and narrow 0.05ºC bracket (1.10ºC to 1.14ºC). While this temperature range is plausible given a weak La Niña / neutral ENSO state transitioning down from the extreme highs of 2023-2024, the available Polymarket price on October 16, 2025, was 4%. By mid-October, sophisticated traders already have access to real-time temperature reanalysis data (like ERA5) for the first half of the month, meaning this 4% likelihood heavily incorporates the actual atmospheric conditions observed during the forecast period. It is safest to closely anchor to this market consensus rather than independently guessing the narrow bracket. I will forecast 4.5% to account for slight remaining uncertainty through the end of the month.
